authority: Official Haydenshapes model descriptions and governed Quivrr catalogue records

models.reason: Modernised single-fin platform proven in powerful surf, with hold and control as the defining role.
Everyday performance model positioned above the Hypto Krypto while retaining broad usability and paddle support.
Explicit everyday-driver intent with Hypto speed and added performance rocker.
Construction variant of the Holy Hypto; construction does not change the underlying family.
Haydenshapes explicitly positions it as a versatile one-board quiver from small beach breaks to solid barrels.
Small-wave performance is the primary design role despite broader everyday usability.
High-speed everyday small-wave driver designed around wave count, flow and accessible performance.
Full-plan fish-derived shape focused on trim, speed and fun in weak surf.
Shorter, wider progressive hybrid with broad everyday usability rather than fish-first intent.
Fish-derived but explicitly designed as a compact small-wave performance board.
Explicit step-up design for 5–12ft+ surf with paddle support and hold.
High-performance shortboard with added speed in weaker conditions.
Updated team-spec high-performance shortboard for progressive surfing across varied conditions.

review_notes: Construction variants retain the design family of their base model.
Merlot was moved from the broad hybrid lane to Groveller because small-wave performance is its primary manufacturer intent.
Plunder remains Fish because trim, planing area and fish-derived speed define the model more strongly than general daily use.
